Can you read that font?

A simple guide for fonts in WCAG

Anna Kocaj

2/18/2026 · 1 min read

Intro

I believe every designer should follow these guidlines for fonts. Sizes, colors and contrasts make all of our lifes easier. I’ll try to explain it in a graphic, easy way. If you’d like to check the official guidelines, here’s a list of what this post is based on:

Criterias applying to fonts in WCAG 2.2

  • 1.4.3 & 1.4.6 Contrast
  • 1.4.4 Resize Text
  • 1.4.5 Images of Text
  • 1.4.8 Visual Presentation
  • 1.4.9 Images of Text (No Exception)
  • 1.4.12 Text Spacing

Sizes

Points, pixels, rem, em, %… there are a lot of units out there. At web desing, we usually use px or rem, but W3C gives us points. So, what to do with that?

Choose the right size

At least 18 point or 14 point bold

All right, let’s do some math. To change points to pixels we should multiply by 1,333 (for standard desktop resolutions 96dpi)

18pt x 1,333 = 24px

14pt x 1,333 =

big text / normal text points into pixels

Let users make it BIG

resizing

Text Spacing

Text Spacing Editor - tool

Colors

Make the contrast work

What if background changes?

opacity background how to measure on videos? take the least contrast pixel from the video
